etymology.description: Depicts two entangled ropes -> to entangle; to intertwine -> original form of įģū(įģū) (jiÅŦ, "to correct")
etymology.interpretation: The character äļĐ(äļĐ) (jiÅŦ, "twist") visually represents two ropes intertwining, naturally conveying the concept of twisting or entangling, and later evolved into įģū(įģū) with a slightly different meaning.
